observed.excerpt: The finding's 'none are authority citations' characterization does NOT hold at 2624d2c7: structured foreign edges reference frozen .project items as standing referents. (1) .pi-context-registry.json registers .project as "sub-0c813fd84348d4c2" with alias "project". (2) .context/relations.json holds 37 foreign edges (relation_type session_touches_item, SESSION-001..005) whose child endpoints carry substrate_id sub-0c813fd84348d4c2 — .project items FGAP-151..182, TASK-094/095, VER-066, DEC-0049 (landed commit 5cfae69a 2026-05-30, '.project frozen, .context canonical going forward... 35 session_touches_item edges'). (3) The per-arc substrate .context-jit-spec-v2/relations.json holds 30 foreign edges to .project: 18 friction_item_files_to_gap (FB-001..FB-018 filing to standing .project gaps FGAP-151..168 etc.) and 12 task_depends_on_task (dependencies on .project items incl. FGAP-115) — landed commit b8a0422c 2026-05-30 ('FB↔FGAP cross-substrate references established via sentinel form'). (4) .context-jit-spec-v2/tasks.json TASK-001 pulls a .project verification forward as attestation: 'FGAP-115 closed in .project with closing-citation + VER-067 attests (project:VER-067)'. These are grounding/dependency/attestation references honoring the frozen substrate, not removal citations. The deprecation/cleanup class the finding sampled does still exist (issue-012 resolved by TASK-108 replacing code-comment .project-era ID citations per the FGAP-136 standard) but targets id-collision opacity in CODE comments — the profile confirms .context reuses FGAP-/DEC- ids for unrelated content, so bare-id code citations were genuinely misdirecting — a different surface than substrate-block referencing. FEAT-012 'Cross-substrate consumption' is status 'proposed': derivation surfaces do not yet consume foreign endpoints, so referenceability is filed-and-traversable but not derivation-consumed (static read; no runtime exercised).

(c) the onboarding accept-all default'; DEC-0043 (enacted) 'config.installed_schemas / installed_blocks enumerate BLOCK-KIND assets ONLY: the user-vocabulary block schemas + starter block files installProject copies from the samples catalog'. Current live substrate enacts the replacement: .context/config.json installed_schemas has 17 entries (decisions, framework-gaps, tasks, ... milestone) and installed_blocks 17; packages/pi-context/samples/schemas/ ships 18 schema files. Count divergence tolerated and recorded: DEC-0043 projected '17 -> 15' symmetric installed sets; live config shows 17/17 (session-notes: '17 block kinds (16 packaged + session-notes)') — substance (catalog-install model populated and operative) stands. Nuance: DEC-0011's opt-in kernel survives — install/accept-all remain explicit ceremonies (commit 53ebe390 2026-05-04 '/project install opt-in mechanism replaces auto-shipped defaults'); what is superseded is the ships-no-seed directional choice itself: the package now ships a full samples catalog as the onboarding accept-all default per DEC-0037. This is a deliberate replacement, matching the finding; confirm-only, nothing to restore absent a contrary ruling.

observed.excerpt: DEC-0047's human-ratification-of-capability-mutation intent is enacted in current code, beyond the 'survives in spirit' the finding recorded. (1) The registry surfaces EXIST (not absent) in .context/config.json: tool_operations (0 entries) and tool_operations_forbidden ([]) — emptiness matches DEC-0047's own operative text, 'config + schemas + macros are the registry of what is grantable (default empty)', so present-but-empty is the decision's stated default, not a gap. (2) The ratification chokepoint exists: author-tool-grant-tool.ts — 'Add or remove an entry in config.tool_operations[] or config.tool_operations_forbidden[]. Requires user authorization via interactive confirmation at the pi-dispatch auth-gate; on confirm, the verified terminal-operator identity is stamped as writer. Refuses any attempt to register a framework-forbidden wholesale token.' (3) auth-gate.ts registered on pi.on('tool_call'): 'the operator authorizes at the terminal, and on confirm=true the handler stamps event.input.writer with a verified terminal-operator identity (overriding whatever the caller supplied)'; non-interactive context (ctx.hasUI === false) -> 'unconditional refusal'. (4) Enforcement consumes the registry: composite-loader.ts:144 'new Set<string>([...FORBIDDEN_WHOLESALE_OPERATIONS, ...(config?.tool_operations_forbidden ?? [])])'. Landing commits: ed9f66f7 + 4e722f06 (2026-05-28, author-tool-grant tool + config schema tool_operations_forbidden[]); 7be299bf, e6a17997, d872fceb (2026-05-29, auth-gate library, wiring at extension factory intercepting gated tools regardless of caller-supplied writer.kind, verified-identity module). Correction recorded: no literal 'constitutional'/'ratification'-named registry exists, and DEC-0047 itself calls that framing 'thinking-scaffolding'; the operative form is the config registry + auth-gate, which is present. Limit: gate behavior judged from a static source read; not runtime-exercised here.
observed.method: python3 json scan of .project/decisions.json printing full DEC-0047 decision text; python3 json scan of .context/config.json keys and tool_operations/tool_operations_forbidden contents; grep -rn 'tool_operations_forbidden|writer.kind|WriterIdentity|constitutional|ratification|audit-substrate-currency|grant.gated' across packages/*/src and .context; sed -n 1,60p packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/auth-gate.ts; git log -S 'author-tool-grant' / -S 'auth-gate' -- packages/pi-agent-dispatch
